title: waltheria involucrata
intro: Waltheria involucrata, also known as Waltheria or Sarsaparilla, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa and Asia, characterized by its sprawling stems and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, Waltheria involucrata has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Waltheria involucrata include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate rheumatic pain. Waltheria involucrata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
